title: Tier 3 — Sécurité Avancée, Architecture et Algorithmes Complexes
prompt:
You are taking a TIER 3 EXPERT evaluation — advanced security, architecture and complex algorithms.

Solve 6 exercises. Return your answers in Markdown using headers for the exercise IDs and code blocks for the JavaScript functions.

[3-A] PowerShell script deploy-patch.ps1 with Copy-Item backup + sqlite3 + rollback on error
[3-B] JavaScript function remplirMatrice(grille, x, y, nouvelleValeur) — Flood Fill with grille[y][x] (x=column, y=row)
[3-C] Async JavaScript function middleware(request, env) — check Bearer SYSTEM-SECURE-KEY header, return 403 if missing/invalid
[3-D] Function rechercherUtilisateurSecurise(db, nomUtilisateur) — parameterized SQL query (use ? placeholder, never string concatenation)
[3-E] Async function executerAvecRetry(operation, maxTentatives) — retry with exponential backoff
[3-F] Fix fusionnerConfig — must be safe against prototype pollution (__proto__ key)

Expected strict response format:
{
  "tache_3a": "<powershell script here>",
  "tache_3b": "function remplirMatrice(grille, x, y, nouvelleValeur) { ... }",
  "tache_3c": "async function middleware(request, env) { ... }",
  "tache_3d": "function rechercherUtilisateurSecurise(db, nomUtilisateur) { ... }",
  "tache_3e": "async function executerAvecRetry(operation, maxTentatives) { ... }",
  "tache_3f": "function fusionnerConfig(...) { ... }"
}

[ALGORITHMIC EXERCISES — code pur, sans culture générale]
Return each function using a Markdown header (### <exercise id>) followed by a ```javascript code block.

[EXERCISE algo_facile_1]
Write a JavaScript function "validerParentheses(s)" that returns true if the parentheses in s are balanced (only "(" and ")" are considered).

[EXERCISE algo_facile_2]
Write a JavaScript function "fibonacci(n)" that returns the n-th Fibonacci number, with fibonacci(0)=0 and fibonacci(1)=1.

[EXERCISE algo_moyen_1]
Write a JavaScript function "motsUniques(s)" that returns the number of distinct words (separated by whitespace) in the string.

[EXERCISE algo_difficile_1]
Write a JavaScript function "aplatirTableau(tab)" that flattens an array of arrays by one level (e.g. [[1,2],[3]] -> [1,2,3]).

[EXERCISE algo_defi]
Write a JavaScript function "rotationDroite(tab, k)" that returns the array rotated right by k positions (e.g. [1,2,3,4], 1 -> [4,1,2,3]).

evaluations.setup: var _sqlUtilise = null; var _paramsUtilises = null; var db = { query: function(sql, params) { _sqlUtilise = sql; _paramsUtilises = params; return [{ id: 1, nom: "test" }]; } };
var db = { query: function(sql, params) { return [{ id: 1, nom: "test" }]; } };

tasks.label: Algorithmique — Parenthèses valides
tasks.hint: Compteur +1 pour '(' et -1 pour ')' ; invalide si négatif ou non nul à la fin.

tasks.label: Algorithmique — Fibonacci
tasks.hint: fib(0)=0, fib(1)=1, fib(n)=fib(n-1)+fib(n-2). Itérez pour éviter la complexité exponentielle.

tasks.label: Algorithmique — Mots uniques
tasks.hint: Construisez un Set des mots (split sur les espaces) et renvoyez sa taille.

tasks.label: Algorithmique — Aplatir un tableau
tasks.hint: Utilisez .flat(1), ou concaténez chaque sous-tableau avec .concat / spread.

tasks.label: Algorithmique — Rotation à droite
tasks.hint: tab.slice(-k).concat(tab.slice(0, -k)) effectue une rotation droite de k.
